name: Els Arcs (Circular PR-CV 49 & 151)
region_intro: Hiking around Castell De Castells offers exploration of a dramatic mountainous landscape in the Marina Alta interior of Alicante. The region features rugged limestone mountains, winding valleys, and notable ravines. Trails lead through varied terrain, from tranquil forest paths to ancient stone terraces and challenging rocky sections, providing panoramic viewpoints of the surrounding valleys and the Guadalest Reservoir. Best hiking trails around Castell De Castells The most popular hiking route is Els Arcs (Circular PR-CV 49 & 151), a 3.3 miles (5.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 40 minutes to complete. This route leads to the impressive natural rock formations known as Els Arcs. Another top favourite among local hikers is Portet de Castells – View of Guadalest Reservoir loop from Castell de Castells, a difficult 5.9 miles (9.5 km) path. This trail offers commanding views of the Guadalest Reservoir and surrounding peaks. Local hikers also love the La Venta del Collao Restaurant – Collado de Garga Viewpoint loop from Pla de Petracos, a 6.1 miles (9.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes. Hiking around Castell De Castells is defined by rugged limestone mountains, deep ravines, and ancient stone terraces. The network offers options for easy walks to challenging ascents. The routes in Castell De Castells are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 2800 hikers have used komoot to explore Castell De Castells's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Castell de Castells?
A: There are over 45 hiking trails around Castell de Castells, offering a diverse range of experiences from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain will I encounter on hikes in Castell de Castells?
A: The terrain in Castell de Castells is characterized by rugged limestone mountains, deep ravines, and winding valleys. You can expect varied paths, including tranquil forest sections, ancient stone terraces, and some challenging rocky ascents. Many routes also feature panoramic viewpoints.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Castell de Castells?
A: Yes, Castell de Castells offers several easy routes suitable for families or those seeking a less strenuous walk. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23524926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almond Orchards – Start of the Els Arcs route loop from PR-CV 151: Els Arcs&lt;/a&gt; is an easy trail that takes you through pleasant landscapes. The region also features natural springs like Font de la Retura, which are ideal for families.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see while hiking?
A: A must-see natural wonder is &lt;strong&gt;Els Arcs (The Arches)&lt;/strong&gt;, spectacular natural rock formations. You can explore them on routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e870152348/els-arcs-circular-pr-cv-49-151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Arcs (Circular PR-CV 49 & 151)&lt;/a&gt;. The area also boasts dramatic peaks, ravines, and numerous panoramic viewpoints offering vistas of the surrounding valleys and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/73676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites accessible via hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. You can visit the highly significant prehistoric site of &lt;strong&gt;Pla de Petracos&lt;/strong&gt;, which features 7,000-year-old Neolithic rock paintings. The ruins of a Moorish Watchtower, also known as Penya Castellet, perched high on a hilltop, offer historical intrigue and commanding views.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Castell de Castells?
A: The trails in Castell de Castells are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 600 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning mountain scenery, the well-marked paths, and the diverse terrain that offers something for everyone.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many trails in Castell de Castells are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1027179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Arches – Els Arcs — Natural Rock Arches loop from PR-CV 151: Els Arcs&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through varied landscapes to the impressive natural arches.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Castell de Castells?
A: The best time for hiking in Castell de Castells is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, making for comfortable hiking conditions. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Is there parking available for hikers in Castell de Castells?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around the village of Castell de Castells, as well as at the starting points of many popular trails. Specific parking details can often be found on individual route descriptions.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or natural springs to discover?
A: While the region is known for its rugged mountains, you can find refreshing natural springs like Font de la Retura. For waterfalls, nearby attractions include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/398248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algar Springs Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2889417" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall in Barranco de l'Encantada&lt;/a&gt;, which are worth exploring.

Q: Can I find accommodation or places to eat in Castell de Castells?
A: The remote mountain village of Castell de Castells offers a peaceful, authentic rural charm. You can find local establishments for food and drink, and there are options for accommodation, including rural hotels and guesthouses, providing a comfortable base after a day of hiking.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, Castell de Castells offers several challenging routes for experienced hikers. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38257381" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portet de Castells – View of Guadalest Reservoir loop from Castell de Castells&lt;/a&gt; provide significant elevation gain and rugged sections, rewarding trekkers with extensive views and a demanding experience.
tips: Els Arcs are the arch formations that have been produced due to the erosion of the limestone rock. This natural monument is located in the northern part of the Serra de la Aixortá.
